name: PULMONARY ARTERIAL MALFORMATION
definition: PULMONARY ARTERIOVENOUS ANEURYSM = PAVM PULMONARY ARTERIOVENOUS FISTULA = PULMONARY ARTERIOVENOUS MALFORMATION = abnormal vascular communication between pulmonary artery and vein (95%) or systemic

demographics.Age: 3rd-4th decade; manifest in adult life, 10% in childhood
demographics.Location: lower lobes (60-70%) > middle lobe > upper lobes; bilateral (8-20%); medial third

dominant_finding: "coin lesion" = sharply defined, lobulated oval / round mass (90%) of 1 to several cm in size
distribution: lower lobes (60-70%) > middle lobe > upper lobes; bilateral (8-20%); medial third
discriminating_features: "coin lesion" = sharply defined, lobulated oval / round mass (90%) of 1 to several cm in size
cordlike bands from mass to hilum (= feeding artery + draining veins)
decrease: expiration, Valsalva maneuver, recumbent position

general: "coin lesion" = sharply defined, lobulated oval / round mass (90%) of 1 to several cm in size
cordlike bands from mass to hilum (= feeding artery + draining veins)
decrease: expiration, Valsalva maneuver, recumbent position
increase: inspiration, Müller maneuver (= forced inspiration against a closed glottis after
homogeneous circumscribed noncalcified nodule / serpiginous mass up to several cm in
vascular connection of mass with enlarged feeding artery + draining vein
sequential enhancement of feeding artery + aneurysmal part + efferent vein on dynamic CT
irregular T2-hypointense lesion, usually adjacent to hilum
signal void on standard spin echo / high SI on GRASS images

clinical.Cx: CNS symptoms are commonly the initial manifestation
